title: helianthemum × lagunae
intro: Helianthemum × lagunae is a hybrid plant species, resulting from the cross between Helianthemum alpestre and Helianthemum nummularium, both members of the rockrose family (Cistaceae). It is known for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, providing relief from pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Helianthemum × lagunae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it accessible for those seeking to harness its medicinal benefits.
